title: Assistant Manager (Accounts Payable)
description:
Accounts Payable
• Manage the full accounts payable cycle.
• Lead and supervise the AP team to ensure compliance with accounting policies and SOPs.
• Ensure purchase documents are complete, properly approved, and accurately recorded on time.
• Ensure timely and accurate submission of withholding tax (WHT).
• Maintain complete and accurate records of agent commissions and royalties.
• Complete month-end and year-end closing activities within required timelines.
• Work closely with the GL team and follow up on bank reconciliation items.
• Approve new vendor creation in Navision.
• Guide PO users and approvers on proper use of the Navision system.

Cash Flow Management
• Prepare monthly cash flow forecasts and projections.
• Support effective cash flow management and liquidity planning.
• Monitor fund availability to ensure timely payments.
• Coordinate with operations on expected cash collections and manage outstanding payments.

Management Reporting
• Ensure timely month-end and year-end closing of the AP sub-ledger.
• Ensure expenses are fully and accurately recorded.
• Prepare AP reports for month-end and year-end closing.
• Perform expense variance analysis for management reporting.
• Prepare and review monthly audit schedules.
• Compile financial data for board presentations.

Forecasting & Budgeting
• Assist in preparing forecasts and budgets for direct costs and operating expenses.

Outsourced Vendor Management
• Oversee performance of outsourced vendors.
• Ensure vendors meet daily and required deliverables.
• Provide regular feedback and performance guidance.
• Ensure Desktop Procedures are updated as required.

Compliance (EduTrust & Audit)
• Ensure compliance with EduTrust and audit requirements related to payment controls.
• Review and update documentation to meet EduTrust (CPE) standards.
• Support audit processes and liaise with external auditors on audit requests.

Business Support & Issue Resolution
• Resolve operational issues and act as a finance business partner to business units.
• Handle correspondence and follow up on internal and external queries.

Fixed Assets
• Ensure fixed assets are accurately recorded and properly tagged when required.

Staff Management
• Lead, motivate, and develop the AP and outsourced teams to improve efficiency.
• Conduct performance appraisals and provide coaching and training.

Others
• Perform other ad-hoc duties as assigned by the company.

We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. By submitting your application, you agree to the collection, use, retention, and disclosure of your personal information to prospective employers.

postedCompany.description:
Wecruit is one of the leading recruitment firms based in Singapore, dedicated to helping job seekers find the right career opportunities.


We partner with reputable companies across a wide range of industries, including Healthcare, IT, Corporate Support, Engineering, Education, Logistics & Supply Chain, Oil & Gas, and Maritime. 


Whether you're looking for permanent roles or short-term assignments, our consultants take the time to understand your experience, preferences, and goals to match you with suitable roles.

With over 1,000 successful placements and a strong track record of candidate satisfaction, Wecruit is here to support your career journey.


Let us help you take the next step—reach out to explore current opportunities or to speak with our team.
